article_items.title: A classic brain test exposed AI's biggest weakness
article_items.description: Researchers gave top AI models a classic attention test used in psychology and found a major flaw. While the models could correctly name colors in short lists, their performance deteriorated sharply as the task became longer and more complex. Some leading systems fell from over 90% accuracy to nearly complete failure.
article_items.excerpt: Researchers gave top AI models a classic attention test used in psychology and found a major flaw. While the models could correctly name colors in short lists, their performance deteriorated sharply as the task became longer and more complex. Some leading systems fell from over 90% accuracy to nearly complete failure.
article_items.summary: When the list expanded to forty words, accuracy dropped to just 15%. They are trained on enormous amounts of text and learn patterns in language, allowing them to generate responses that often appear remarkably human. According to the researchers, the AI models had trouble maintaining the instruction to identify ink colors. key_points: When the list expanded to forty words, accuracy dropped to just 15%.
According to the researchers, the AI models had trouble maintaining the instruction to identify ink colors.
Sometimes the word and the ink color match.
The study highlights an important distinction between human and artificial intelligence.
The researchers argue that the performance collapse seen in these experiments points to fundamental limitations in today's large language models.
The researchers wanted to see whether modern large language models (LLMs) handle this challenge in the same way humans do.
The brain must suppress the urge to read the word and instead focus on identifying the ink color.
The researchers observed similar patterns in GPT-5, Claude Opus 4.1, and Gemini 2.5.
They are trained on enormous amounts of text and learn patterns in language, allowing them to generate responses that often appear remarkably human.
While the models could correctly name colors in short lists, their performance deteriorated sharply as the task became longer and more complex.
Participants are asked to name the color of the ink rather than read the word itself.
Although modern AI systems can produce impressive language and reasoning capabilities, their underlying mechanisms differ from the attention processes found in biological brains.

article_items.title: Anthropic's Fable AI brings the capabilities of its unreleased Mythos model to regular users - Engadget
article_items.description: Claude subscribers can try the model until June 22 without spending usage credits.
article_items.excerpt: Claude subscribers can try the model until June 22 without spending usage credits.
article_items.summary: "With more capable models arriving in the coming months, we're working to improve our safeguards and reduce false positives as quickly as we can." On Tuesday, Anthropic also released Mythos 5, which runs on the same underlying model as Fable 5 but comes with fewer safeguards. The model Anthropic used for that Twitch series, 3.7 Sonnet , required an overlay to allow Claude to make sense of the game's pixel art interface and world. "To release [Fable 5] both safely and quickly, we've tuned these safeguards conservatively - they'll sometimes catch harmless requests, though they trigger, on average, in less than 5 percent of sessions," the company said. Anthropic says the new system, which starts at version five to align with the company's other models, offers capabilities that "exceed" those of any it has made publicly available in the past. "The longer and more complex the task, the larger Fable 5's lead over our other models," Anthropic says.

Anthropic's Fable AI brings the capabilities of its unreleased Mythos model to regular users Claude subscribers can try the model until June 22 without spending usage credits.
Anthropic has just announced Fable , the start of a new family of models that brings many of the capabilities of its Mythos system to the public.

"To release [Fable 5] both safely and quickly, we've tuned these safeguards conservatively - they'll sometimes catch harmless requests, though they trigger, on average, in less than 5 percent of sessions," the company.
Last week, Anthropic said it was working as quickly as possible to release a Mythos-capable model with "highly robust safeguards" to the public, and today it's doing exactly that with Fable.
The project saw Anthropic share access to the model with select partners, including Apple and NVIDIA, with the aim of helping those organizations harden their software against AI cyberattacks.
Claude subscribers can try the model until June 22 without spending usage credits.
As a refresher, Mythos is the state-of-the-art model Anthropic debuted at the start of April through Project Glasswing .
"After this point - when sufficient capacity allows us to do so - we aim to restore Fable 5 as a standard part of subscription plans.
Claude subscribers can try Fable 5 until June 22.

article_items.title: Nvidia and Hyundai deepen AI partnership in robotics and mobility
article_items.description: Nvidia and Hyundai Motor Group have agreed to deepen their collaboration in artificial intelligence, robotics, and future mobility technologies as the two companies seek to accelerate the commercialization of physical AI and expand South Korea's role in next-generation AI infrastructure.
